reason: Placeholder — l0 and l1 extract non-overlapping ranges so same l0 doesn't constrain l1; replaced with a real test below

name: l0_bits_covers_9_bits
reason: When all 9 bits (39-47) are set, l0_bits should return 511 (2^9 - 1) — tests that the extraction range is exactly 9 bits wide, not wider or narrower

name: l3_bits_zero_below_page_boundary
reason: Addresses below 4096 (one page) should have zero l3_bits since bits 12-20 are all zero — tests that sub-page offsets don't pollute the L3 index

name: bit_ranges_non_overlapping
reason: The four bit extraction ranges (L0: 39-47, L1: 30-38, L2: 21-29, L3: 12-20) must be disjoint — overlapping ranges would cause one level's index to be influenced by another level's bits

name: all_index_bits_reconstruct_addr
reason: Reassembling the four 9-bit indices (shifted back) should recover bits 12-47 of the original address — if not, the extraction macros are losing or corrupting address information used for page table indexing

name: bits_ignore_high_48_plus
reason: l0_bits extracts bits 39-47 so should be unaffected by bits 48-63 — tests that non-canonical high bits don't influence the L0 page table index
